title: herniaria vulgaris var. glabrescens
intro: Herniaria vulgaris var. glabrescens, also known as glabrous common sneezewort, is a variety of the common sneezewort plant, a low-growing, annual herb native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Herniaria vulgaris var. glabrescens include its ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues, reduce congestion, and alleviate symptoms of allergies and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
